name: Turtle Protein Cheesecake
description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11.5 cal/g protein (~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

payload.instructionText: Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.
